name: 若斯坎·德普雷
brief: 若斯坎出生于法国-佛兰德斯地区，是文艺复兴时期最伟大的作曲家之一。他曾在米兰、罗马教廷和法国宫廷任职，被同时代人称为「音乐家中的王者」。马丁·路德曾赞叹：「若斯坎是音符的主人，音符必须听命于他；其他作曲家则必须听命于音符。」他的作品在整个欧洲广泛传播，印刷术的发明使他成为第一位享有国际声誉的作曲家。
briefEn: Born in the Franco-Flemish region, Josquin was one of the greatest composers of the Renaissance. He held positions in Milan, at the Papal Chapel in Rome, and at the French royal court, earning the title 'prince of musicians' among his contemporaries. Martin Luther famously declared that Josquin was the master of the notes, which had to do as he willed, while other composers had to do as the notes willed. Thanks to the advent of music printing, his works circulated throughout Europe, making him the first composer to achieve true international fame.
contribution: 若斯坎将佛兰德复调传统推向了新的高度，他在弥撒曲和经文歌中实现了文字与音乐的深度融合——音乐不再仅是礼仪的装饰，而成为表达文本情感的工具。他开创了「模仿式复调」（Imitative Counterpoint）的写作手法，各声部依次进入，构成精妙的对话，这一技法成为后续两百年复调写作的基石。
contributionEn: Josquin elevated the Franco-Flemish polyphonic tradition to new heights, achieving a profound union of text and music in his masses and motets — music was no longer mere liturgical ornament but became a vehicle for expressing the emotions of the text. He pioneered the technique of imitative counterpoint, in which voices enter one after another in carefully crafted dialogue, a method that would remain the cornerstone of polyphonic composition for the next two centuries.
style: 若斯坎的音乐兼具理性的精密与感性的表达。他的复调织体清晰透明，每个声部既独立又和谐统一。他善于运用留白与静默制造戏剧效果，在密集的对位段落之间穿插简洁的主调和声，形成呼吸般的节奏感。他的音乐既有宗教的庄严，也有人文主义的温暖。
styleEn: Josquin's music unites intellectual precision with expressive depth. His polyphonic textures are luminously clear, each voice maintaining its independence while blending into a harmonious whole. He wielded silence and space to striking dramatic effect, alternating dense contrapuntal passages with moments of simple homophony to create a natural sense of breathing. His music possesses both sacred gravity and humanistic warmth.

famousScores.name: 《圣母颂》 Ave Maria...virgo serena
famousScores.desc: 四声部经文歌，若斯坎最著名的作品，史上第一本印刷经文歌集的开篇之作，模仿式复调的典范
famousScores.descEn: A four-voice motet and Josquin's most famous work — the opening piece of the first printed motet anthology (1502) and a textbook example of imitative counterpoint
famousScores.detail:
《圣母颂》约创作于1480年代，彼时若斯坎正效力于米兰斯福尔扎公爵宫廷。作品以天使报喜的礼仪诗文为词，若斯坎为每一拉丁诗节量身设计了独特的音乐动机，四个声部依次以模仿方式进入，如精妙的对话般交织回荡。这种将词义与音型深度绑定的写法，是若斯坎对复调音乐最重要的革新之一：音乐不再是礼仪装饰，而成为传递文字情感的载体。

1502年，威尼斯印刷商奥塔维亚诺·彼得鲁奇将这首经文歌置于史上第一部印刷经文歌集《经文歌集·甲》的首位——在音乐印刷史揭幕的那一刻，他选择了若斯坎作为代言人。随后一个世纪里，这首作品被反复翻印与手抄，在欧洲各地广泛流传，成为文艺复兴时代单一流传最广的声乐作品，也是当时作曲家们争相研习的范本。

在音乐史上，《圣母颂》确立了模仿式复调的范式：四个声部以同一主题依次进入、彼此呼应，构成精妙的声部网络。这一手法被帕莱斯特里纳、拉索和伯德奉为圭臬，成为16世纪欧洲复调写作的核心技法。19世纪音乐学复兴时，这首作品再度成为若斯坎天才的最佳证明，象征着文艺复兴音乐的纯粹理想。
famousScores.detailEn:
Ave Maria...virgo serena was almost certainly composed during Josquin's tenure at the Sforza court in Milan in the 1480s. Setting a text drawn from the Annunciation liturgy, Josquin devised a distinct melodic motive for each Latin phrase, with all four voices entering in overlapping imitation to create an intricately woven polyphonic tapestry. This close binding of musical idea to textual meaning was one of Josquin's most revolutionary innovations: music was no longer liturgical decoration but had become a vehicle for emotional communication.

In 1502, the Venetian printer Ottaviano Petrucci placed this motet as the opening work of Motetti A, the first printed motet anthology in history. As a new chapter in music's transmission began, Petrucci chose Josquin to speak first. Over the following century, the motet was reprinted and hand-copied countless times across Europe, making it the single most widely disseminated vocal piece of the Renaissance and a model eagerly studied by composers everywhere.

Musically, Ave Maria established the paradigm of imitative counterpoint — successive vocal entries on a shared melodic idea, voices answering one another in cascading dialogue — that became the central technique of sixteenth-century polyphony. Palestrina, Lasso, and Byrd all absorbed this language from Josquin's motets. When nineteenth-century scholars began rediscovering early music, this motet was once again the piece they cited first as proof of Josquin's genius and as the purest embodiment of the Renaissance musical ideal.

famousScores.name: 《赞颂舌头弥撒》 Missa Pange lingua
famousScores.desc: 基于圣体节赞美诗的释义弥撒，被公认为若斯坎最伟大的弥撒曲，可能也是他最后的弥撒
famousScores.descEn: A paraphrase mass on a Corpus Christi hymn by Thomas Aquinas, widely regarded as Josquin's greatest mass and possibly his last
famousScores.detail:
《赞颂舌头弥撒》被普遍认为是若斯坎最后的弥撒曲，可能创作于1510年代，时值他晚年定居孔德（Condé-sur-l'Escaut）之后。这是一首「释义弥撒」（Paraphrase Mass），以托马斯·阿奎那为圣体节创作的赞美诗《赞颂舌头》（Pange lingua）为基础。不同于「固定旋律弥撒」将原曲旋律原封不动置于某一声部，若斯坎将这首赞美诗的旋律拆解、变形、分散于四个声部之间，使整部弥撒如同从同一颗种子生长出来的有机体。

这部弥撒曲在若斯坎生前似乎并未出版，直到1539年才付梓印行。然而它被抄写在多本重要手稿中，说明在作曲家的圈子里早已广为人知。音乐学家们在比较若斯坎所有弥撒曲后，将这部晚期作品列为他风格最成熟、最具深度的代表作——在这里，若斯坎完全超越了技术炫示，以极度内敛的方式表达了最深沉的宗教情感。

《赞颂舌头弥撒》开创了释义弥撒的典范，影响了帕莱斯特里纳在该体裁中的所有创作，整个特伦托会议后的教会弥撒曲写作传统都能追溯到若斯坎这部晚年杰作的影响。它标志着复调弥撒从技术演示走向精神内省的关键转折。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Missa Pange lingua is widely regarded as Josquin's final mass, most likely composed in the 1510s after he retired to Condé-sur-l'Escaut in his native region. It is a paraphrase mass built on the Corpus Christi hymn attributed to Thomas Aquinas. Rather than placing the hymn tune in one voice as a cantus firmus, Josquin dissolved it into all four voices — fragmenting, transforming, and distributing its phrases throughout the texture so that the entire mass seems to grow organically from a single seed.

The work was apparently not published in Josquin's lifetime; it only appeared in print in 1539. Yet it circulated in several important manuscripts, indicating that it was known and admired in musical circles from an early stage. After examining all of Josquin's masses, scholars have consistently identified this late work as the one that best demonstrates his full maturity — here he transcends technical display entirely, expressing the deepest religious emotion through extraordinary economy and restraint.

The Missa Pange lingua established the paraphrase mass as the preeminent genre of the sixteenth century, directly inspiring Palestrina's approach to the form. In a broader sense, the entire post-Tridentine tradition of Catholic mass composition traces its lineage to the expressive ideals embodied in this final masterwork. It marks a decisive turning point in the history of the genre, from technical showcase to spiritual meditation.

famousScores.name: 《武装的人弥撒》 Missa L'homme armé super voces musicales
famousScores.nameEn: Missa L'homme armé super voces musicales
famousScores.desc: 基于流行世俗旋律的弥撒曲，包含大量比例卡农，是对位技法的炫技之作
famousScores.descEn: A mass on the famous secular tune featuring numerous mensuration canons — a dazzling tour de force of contrapuntal virtuosity
famousScores.detail:
《武装的人》是中世纪晚期和文艺复兴时期最流行的世俗曲调之一，约有40位作曲家以此曲为基础创作弥撒曲。若斯坎本人就写了两首以此为题材的弥撒曲，其中「依音阶各音」（super voces musicales）版本被视为其中更具技术挑战性的一首。在这部弥撒中，若斯坎将《武装的人》的曲调依次移调到六种不同的音阶度数（ut、re、mi、fa、sol、la）上演唱，同时在各乐章中嵌入了复杂的比例卡农——即以不同速度比例同时演唱同一旋律的技术。

这部弥撒最令人叹为观止的是「羔羊颂」（Agnus Dei）乐章中的三重卡农：三个声部以不同速度（2:3:1的比例）同时演唱同一旋律，却神奇地和谐共鸣，构成名副其实的数学奇迹。据说若斯坎的同代人认为这样的写法超出了人类智力的极限，将其称为「天使的杰作」。

这部弥撒代表了文艺复兴理性主义美学的顶峰：相信音乐的数学结构与宇宙秩序相通，作曲家通过驾驭复杂的音数关系来体现上帝的秩序之美。这一理念直接影响了后来的巴赫，尤其体现在《音乐的奉献》和《赋格的艺术》中对数学对位的极致追求。
famousScores.detailEn:
The melody L'homme armé ('The Armed Man') was the most popular cantus firmus of the Renaissance, used by some forty composers as the basis for masses. Josquin wrote two masses on this tune; the 'super voces musicales' version is the more technically demanding of the two. In it, Josquin transposes the melody to each of the six syllables of the hexachord (ut, re, mi, fa, sol, la) across successive mass sections, creating an elaborate transposition scheme while simultaneously embedding complex mensuration canons throughout.

The most awe-inspiring moment is the Agnus Dei, where Josquin writes a triple mensuration canon: three voices simultaneously sing the same melody at the rhythmic ratios of 2:3:1, yet miraculously align into perfect harmony. Josquin's contemporaries reportedly regarded this feat as beyond the limits of human intellect, calling it a work worthy of angels. The sheer intellectual audacity of the construction still astonishes musicians and scholars today.

This mass represents the apex of Renaissance rationalist aesthetics — the belief that music's mathematical structure resonates with the order of the cosmos, and that a composer who masters complex numerical relationships is revealing the beauty of divine design. This ideal connects directly to Bach, who pursued similar mathematical contrapuntal perfection in The Musical Offering and The Art of Fugue nearly two centuries later.

famousScores.name: 《上帝怜悯我》 Miserere mei, Deus
famousScores.desc: 五声部经文歌，以反复的固定音型和极简手法表达深沉悲恸，诗篇51的伟大设定
famousScores.descEn: A five-voice motet setting of Psalm 51, conveying profound grief through a recurring ostinato and strikingly austere means
famousScores.detail:
《上帝怜悯我》是若斯坎在费拉拉宫廷任职期间（1503-1504）根据埃尔科莱一世公爵的委托创作的五声部经文歌，以诗篇第51篇为词。这首诗篇是大卫王在犯罪之后向上帝忏悔的祈求，若斯坎为其设计了一个贯穿全曲的固定音型（Ostinato）：「Miserere mei, Deus」这句话以单调、近乎强迫性的方式在各声部中反复出现，有时升高半音，有时降低半音，以音乐的方式表现了灵魂在罪孽重压下的颤抖与哀号。

1503年费拉拉爆发了严重的瘟疫，若斯坎本人亦居于危险之中。有学者认为，这首作品深沉而绝望的情感，部分来自若斯坎对死亡的亲身感受。公爵埃尔科莱一世本人在1505年去世，若斯坎随即离开费拉拉——这首《上帝怜悯我》或许是他在那段充满死亡阴影的岁月里留下的最深刻的音乐见证。

这首作品对后世的影响主要体现在对「极简主义」对位的启发：用最少的音乐素材表达最深沉的情感。固定音型的使用方式预示了两个世纪后帕赫贝尔的卡农低音与巴赫的夏康。它也是文艺复兴经文歌中最早将音乐悲剧性发挥到极致的作品之一，直接影响了拉索《忏悔诗篇》的情感写法。
famousScores.detailEn:
Miserere mei, Deus is a five-voice motet setting Psalm 51, composed during Josquin's tenure at the Este court in Ferrara (1503–1504) at the commission of Duke Ercole I. The psalm is King David's confession and prayer for mercy after his transgression, and Josquin created for it a haunting ostinato: the words 'Miserere mei, Deus' recur with almost obsessive regularity throughout all voices, sometimes rising a semitone, sometimes falling, as if the soul were trembling and groaning under the weight of its guilt.

In 1503, a severe plague swept through Ferrara and Josquin himself was in danger. Some scholars have argued that the motet's overwhelming sense of grief and desperation draws on Josquin's direct confrontation with death. Duke Ercole I died in 1505, shortly after which Josquin departed Ferrara; this motet may be the most vivid musical witness to those years shadowed by mortality.

The work's lasting influence lies in its pioneering of a minimalist approach to counterpoint — expressing the deepest emotion through the most economical means. The use of a recurring ostinato anticipates Pachelbel's canon bass and Bach's chaconne by nearly two centuries. It also stands among the earliest Renaissance motets to push musical tragedy to its extreme, directly inspiring the emotional language of Lasso's Penitential Psalms.

famousScores.name: 《圣母悼歌》 Stabat Mater
famousScores.desc: 五声部经文歌，以Binchois的香颂'Comme femme'为定旋律，文艺复兴声乐经典
famousScores.descEn: A five-voice motet built on the tenor of Binchois's chanson 'Comme femme desconfortée,' a Renaissance vocal masterpiece
famousScores.detail:
若斯坎的五声部《圣母悼歌》以比利时作曲家班舒瓦（Gilles de Bins dit Binchois，约1400-1460）的法语香颂《如同孤苦的女人》（Comme femme desconfortée）的定旋律为基础。以世俗旋律承载宗教经文，是若斯坎惯用的「拼贴」技巧——他将班舒瓦的旋律置于定旋律声部，上方三个声部则演唱中世纪的宗教赞美诗《圣母悼歌》，两层世界在对位中融为一体，世俗的哀愁与神圣的悲悯相互映照。

这种将世俗香颂与宗教经文并置的做法，在今天看来颇为大胆，但在文艺复兴时期的神学语境中却有其深刻含义：圣母玛利亚在十字架前的悲恸，被类比为普通女人在困苦中的哀泣，人性的痛苦与圣母的悲伤在音乐中合而为一。若斯坎的这一写法，正是「人文主义」音乐观的体现——宗教不是高高在上的教条，而是人类情感的升华。

这首作品深刻影响了文艺复兴声乐写作的美学取向，提示了后来作曲家在处理宗教题材时融入人性温暖的可能性。帕莱斯特里纳的《圣母悼歌》虽然风格迥异，却同样秉承了这一将神圣与人性融合的精神。若斯坎的《圣母悼歌》至今仍是文艺复兴合唱团的重要演出曲目，以其深沉的情感表达直达听者心灵。
famousScores.detailEn:
Josquin's five-voice Stabat Mater is built upon the tenor of Comme femme desconfortée, a French chanson by the Franco-Flemish composer Gilles de Bins dit Binchois (c.1400–1460). Using a secular melody as the foundation for sacred text was a characteristic Josquin technique: the Binchois tune is placed in the tenor voice, while the three upper voices sing the medieval Marian hymn Stabat Mater, the two worlds fusing in counterpoint, secular sorrow and sacred grief illuminating each other.

This juxtaposition of secular chanson and sacred text may seem provocative, but within the humanist theology of the Renaissance it carries profound meaning: the Virgin Mary's anguish at the foot of the Cross is likened to the grief of any suffering woman, human sorrow and divine compassion becoming one. Josquin's technique is a declaration of humanist musical values — religion is not remote doctrine but the elevation of human feeling to the divine.

The work significantly influenced the aesthetic direction of Renaissance vocal writing, suggesting to later composers the possibility of infusing sacred music with human warmth. Palestrina's Stabat Mater, though wholly different in style, shares this commitment to uniting the sacred and the human. Josquin's setting remains a staple of the Renaissance choral repertoire and continues to move audiences with the immediacy of its emotional expression.

famousScores.name: 《圣母弥撒》 Missa De beata virgine
famousScores.desc: 六声部弥撒曲，若斯坎流传最广的弥撒，以多首圣母旋律为素材的混合释义弥撒
famousScores.descEn: A six-voice mass, Josquin's most widely circulated mass in his lifetime — a hybrid paraphrase mass drawing on multiple Marian plainchant melodies
famousScores.detail:
《圣母弥撒》是若斯坎所有弥撒曲中流传最广、抄本数量最多的一首，这一事实在他生前的知名度可见一斑。这首六声部弥撒曲约作于1510年前后，创作时正值若斯坎职业生涯的高峰期。若斯坎从几首不同的圣歌旋律中提取素材：常规弥撒经文使用一套旋律，而与圣母相关的经文则使用专属的玛利亚旋律，使整部弥撒呈现出统一中有变化的精妙设计。

这部弥撒的一个著名细节是「荣耀经」和「信经」中使用了六个声部，而「慈悲经」和「羔羊颂」则精简为四至五个声部——这种声部数量的灵活调度，使音乐具有明暗交替的戏剧效果，也体现了若斯坎对音乐织体动态平衡的高度敏感。此弥撒曲在16世纪广泛流传于欧洲各地的教堂，从西班牙到波兰均有演唱记录。

《圣母弥撒》代表了若斯坎对「非统一定旋律弥撒」这一混合体裁的最成熟探索，即在同一部弥撒中自由调度多种旋律素材。这一写法预示了帕莱斯特里纳晚期弥撒曲更加灵活的结构处理，在弥撒曲体裁的演变史上占有重要地位。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Missa De beata virgine was Josquin's most widely circulated mass during his lifetime, surviving in more manuscript copies than any other of his masses — a remarkable testament to its popularity. The six-voice work, composed around 1510 at the height of his career, draws on several different plainchant melodies: ordinary mass texts are set to one set of chants, while sections whose texts specifically honor the Virgin employ dedicated Marian melodies, creating a design of unified variety.

One notable feature is the flexible handling of vocal forces: the Gloria and Credo are set for six voices, while the Kyrie and Agnus Dei are reduced to four or five — a dynamic variation that creates a natural sense of light and shade and reveals Josquin's exceptional sensitivity to textural balance. Records of the mass being sung survive from churches across Europe, from Spain to Poland, testifying to its pan-European reach throughout the sixteenth century.

The Missa De beata virgine represents Josquin's most mature exploration of the hybrid 'non-unified cantus firmus mass,' in which multiple plainchant sources are freely combined within a single work. This approach anticipates the more flexible structural handling found in Palestrina's later masses and marks an important transitional point in the evolution of the genre.

famousScores.name: 《武装的人弥撒（第六调式）》 Missa L'homme armé sexti toni
famousScores.desc: 若斯坎的第二首武装的人弥撒，以第六调式（F大调）写成，风格宏大开阔，情感表达更为直接
famousScores.descEn: Josquin's second L'homme armé mass, cast in the sixth mode (F major), grander and more direct in expression than its mathematically complex companion
famousScores.detail:
若斯坎的第二首《武装的人弥撒》——「第六调式」（sexti toni）版——与前一首同名弥撒相比，风格截然不同。这首弥撒以第六调式（利底亚调式，相当于今天的F大调）写成，整体风格更为宏大开阔，情感表达更为直接，对位技法相对朴素，不追求前一首中密集的数学炫示。有学者认为这是若斯坎早年的作品，另一些人则认为两首几乎同期创作，代表了若斯坎探索同一旋律素材的两种截然不同方式。

这首弥撒最引人入胜的地方，是「荣耀经」中引入了一个极具戏剧性的设计：当众人一同高唱「在最高处荣耀归于神」时，音乐突然爆发出全部声部的宏大和鸣，仿佛整个天堂的合唱同时响起。这种戏剧性的音响设计，在当时是一种前所未见的大胆尝试，深刻影响了后来威尼斯乐派的分合唱（Cori spezzati）传统。

「第六调式」版与「依音阶各音」版的并存，展示了若斯坎在同一题材上追求截然不同美学效果的能力——前者是数学美学的极致，后者是情感表达的直接。两者共同构成文艺复兴弥撒曲多元美学的缩影，对巴洛克和古典时期的弥撒曲创作均产生了深远影响。
famousScores.detailEn:
Josquin's second L'homme armé mass — written in the sixth mode (sexti toni, equivalent to our F major) — presents a strikingly different character from its companion. Where the 'super voces musicales' version is a tour de force of intellectual complexity, this work is more expansive and direct in expression, its counterpoint more straightforward, its emotional language more immediately accessible. Scholars have long debated whether it represents an earlier phase of Josquin's career or whether both masses were written around the same time as complementary aesthetic explorations of the same melody.

The most dramatic moment in the mass is a passage in the Gloria where all voices suddenly erupt in full-throated homophony on the words 'in excelsis' ('in the highest'), a burst of sound suggesting the entire heavenly choir singing together. This theatrical contrast between dense counterpoint and block chords was a bold innovation at the time and significantly influenced the development of the Venetian divided-choir (cori spezzati) tradition.

The coexistence of both L'homme armé masses reveals Josquin's extraordinary range: one a monument of mathematical beauty, the other a statement of direct emotional power. Together they embody the pluralism of Renaissance aesthetic ideals and cast a long shadow over the history of the mass, influencing the approach of both Baroque and Classical composers who grappled with the genre's competing demands of intellectual rigor and emotional immediacy.

famousScores.name: 《奥克冈哀歌》 La Déploration de Johannes Ockeghem
famousScores.desc: 若斯坎为悼念恩师奥克冈而作的五声部哀歌，文艺复兴时期最感人的师生情谊音乐见证
famousScores.descEn: A five-voice lament composed to mourn Josquin's teacher Ockeghem, one of the Renaissance's most moving tributes from pupil to master
famousScores.detail:
1497年，若斯坎的恩师、佛兰德复调大师约翰内斯·奥克冈（Johannes Ockeghem）在波尔多去世。为了悼念这位引领自己走入音乐殿堂的前辈，若斯坎创作了这首哀歌，以弗里亚斯·菲利普的法语哀诗《众缪斯女神，哭泣吧》（Nymphes des bois）为词，整首作品浸透在深沉的哀思之中。若斯坎将奥克冈的名字以谐音的方式嵌入音乐语境，令人动容。

最令后人称道的是，若斯坎在这首作品中采用了奥克冈本人最擅长的对位手法作为致敬：长大的旋律线条、缓慢推进的节奏、深沉的低音部——一切都在模仿奥克冈独特的「哥特式」风格，以此向老师致以最深情的音乐告别。据说作品首演时，在场的音乐家们无不动容，连当时的法国宫廷都为之肃然起敬。

《奥克冈哀歌》是文艺复兴时期「哀悼曲」（Déploration）体裁的最高杰作之一，以深沉的师生情谊赋予了这一体裁最真挚的人文温度。这首作品还具有重要的史学价值：诗词中提到了奥克冈的数位学生，成为今天研究15世纪末音乐家交游网络的珍贵史料。
famousScores.detailEn:
In 1497, Johannes Ockeghem — the great Franco-Flemish master who had served as an artistic model for an entire generation, including Josquin — died in Bordeaux. To honor his elder mentor, Josquin composed this five-voice lament, setting a French poem by Jean Molinet, Nymphes des bois (Nymphs of the Woods), in which the Muses of music are called upon to weep. The work is suffused with grief, and Josquin subtly incorporates allusions to Ockeghem's memory throughout the musical texture.

Most remarkably, Josquin paid homage by consciously imitating Ockeghem's own contrapuntal style: long, flowing melodic lines, slowly unfolding rhythms, and a sonorous depth in the bass voices — all deliberately echoing the older master's distinctive 'Gothic' manner. This mimicry of the deceased's language is one of the most moving acts of stylistic homage in music history. Contemporary reports suggest that the work brought tears even to those at the French royal court where it was first heard.

The Déploration stands as one of the supreme achievements of the Renaissance lament genre. Its deep human warmth — born of genuine grief between teacher and pupil — gives the genre its most authentic voice. The poem also names several of Ockeghem's students by name, making the work a valuable historical document for scholars tracing the networks of late fifteenth-century musical life.

famousScores.name: 《千种遗憾》 Mille regretz
famousScores.desc: 四声部法语香颂，深情忧郁，神圣罗马帝国皇帝查理五世最爱的曲子，被称为「皇帝的歌」
famousScores.descEn: A four-voice chanson of melancholy beauty, so beloved by Holy Roman Emperor Charles V that it was called 'La Canción del Emperador'
famousScores.detail:
《千种遗憾》是若斯坎最著名的世俗歌曲之一，四声部香颂，以忧郁而深情的法语诗为词：「我有千种遗憾离开你，放弃你令我欢喜的面容……」这首歌曲以其简洁的旋律、深沉的情感和精致的和声而闻名，被同时代人视为若斯坎最美丽的香颂之一。神圣罗马帝国皇帝查理五世（1500-1558）对这首歌曲爱不释手，将其称为「皇帝的歌」（La Canción del Emperador）。

西班牙作曲家路易斯·德·纳瓦埃斯（Luis de Narváez）在1538年将其改编为维韦拉琴独奏版，收录在他的《海豚》曲集中，这一版本成为16世纪西班牙最流行的器乐改编曲之一，证明了这首作品在当时的巨大影响力。此后又有多位作曲家将其改编为键盘、琉特琴版本，传播至整个欧洲。

《千种遗憾》代表了若斯坎在世俗音乐创作中与他的宗教作品同样高超的艺术成就。它证明了文艺复兴时期最严肃的复调大师同样能写出令凡人心动的世俗情歌，而且在情感深度上毫不逊色于他最宏大的经文歌。这首小小的香颂在跨越五百年后依然能直达听者心灵，成为若斯坎艺术普遍性的最好证明。
famousScores.detailEn:
Mille regretz ('A Thousand Regrets') is one of Josquin's most celebrated secular songs — a four-voice chanson of melancholy beauty whose text speaks of a thousand regrets at leaving a beloved face, and of the sorrow that shortens life. The piece was treasured by its contemporaries for its lucid melody, deeply felt emotion, and refined harmony. Holy Roman Emperor Charles V (1500–1558) was reportedly captivated by it, calling it 'La Canción del Emperador' — the Emperor's Song.

The Spanish composer Luis de Narváez transcribed the piece for vihuela in his collection Los seys libros del Delphín (1538), and this arrangement became one of the most popular instrumental versions in sixteenth-century Spain. Numerous other composers arranged it for keyboard and lute, spreading its influence across Europe and demonstrating the rare power of a vocal work that could transcend its medium entirely.

Mille regretz reveals that Josquin, the supreme master of sacred polyphony, could write secular love songs of equal emotional depth and lasting power. The song proves that the humanist values driving his motets — music as a vehicle for genuine feeling — were just as operative in his lightest creations. That a tiny chanson of just a few minutes can still move listeners five centuries later is perhaps the most eloquent testimony to Josquin's universal artistic gift.

famousScores.name: 《纯洁完整的圣母》 Inviolata integra et casta es Maria
famousScores.nameEn: Inviolata integra et casta es Maria
famousScores.desc: 五至六声部宏大经文歌，以格里高利序列曲为双重定旋律，规模宏大，技法登峰造极
famousScores.descEn: A monumental five- or six-voice motet with a Gregorian sequence as a recurring ostinato, exceptional in scale and contrapuntal mastery
famousScores.detail:
《纯洁完整的圣母》是若斯坎规模最宏大的经文歌之一，为五声部（或根据版本为六声部）写成，演唱时间可达15分钟甚至更长。若斯坎以格里高利圣歌中专属于圣母节庆的一首序列曲（Sequence）为定旋律，让低音两个声部反复演唱这段旋律，而上方三个声部则自由展开复调织体，形成一个庞大而庄严的音乐建筑。

这首经文歌在技术上展示了若斯坎对「固定音型低音」技法的纯熟掌握——两个低声部在全曲中不断重复同一段定旋律，这与后来巴赫的《帕萨卡利亚》中的固定低音原理如出一辙。16世纪的音乐理论家皮埃特罗·阿龙（Pietro Aaron）曾在他的理论著作中专门引用这首作品作为技术分析的范例，足见其在当时即已受到高度重视。

在内容上，这首经文歌以最精心的音乐语言颂扬了圣母玛利亚的纯洁与崇高，是若斯坎玛利亚崇敬作品系列的重要组成部分。它展示了若斯坎在最大规模的声乐结构中如何保持音乐的逻辑与美感，是他后期成熟风格的极致体现，至今仍是文艺复兴合唱团极具挑战性的演出曲目。
famousScores.detailEn:
Inviolata integra et casta es Maria is one of Josquin's most ambitious motets, written in five or six voices and lasting up to fifteen minutes — an exceptional length for its era. Based on a Gregorian sequence sung on feasts of the Virgin, Josquin assigns its melody to the two lowest voices as a repeating cantus firmus, while the three upper voices weave a freely elaborated polyphonic web above and around it, creating a vast and solemn musical architecture.

The technical mastery on display is astonishing: the two lower voices repeat the same plainchant melody multiple times throughout the piece, a technique analogous to the ostinato bass principles that Bach would employ in his Passacaglia nearly two centuries later. The sixteenth-century music theorist Pietro Aaron cited this very motet in his treatise as a pedagogical example of advanced technique, evidence that it was immediately recognized as a work of exceptional sophistication.

As one of Josquin's great Marian works — alongside Ave Maria and the Missa De beata virgine — the motet expresses his deep and lifelong devotion to the Virgin through music of the highest care and refinement. It demonstrates Josquin's ability to sustain musical logic and beauty across a very large-scale structure, making it a supreme expression of his late mature style and a work that continues to challenge and reward singers and listeners alike.

influence: 若斯坎的创作理念——音乐应当服务于文字的表达——直接影响了帕莱斯特里纳、拉索等后来的复调大师。他的模仿式复调技法经由这些继承者传递到巴洛克时期，最终在巴赫的赋格中达到极致。作为第一位借助印刷术广泛传播作品的作曲家，若斯坎也开创了作曲家个人声誉的概念，改变了音乐创作的社会地位。
influenceEn: Josquin's guiding principle — that music must serve the expression of the text — directly shaped later polyphonic masters such as Palestrina and Lasso. His imitative counterpoint technique passed through these successors into the Baroque era, reaching its ultimate fulfillment in the fugues of Bach. As the first composer whose works were widely disseminated through the printing press, Josquin also pioneered the very concept of the composer as a recognized individual, transforming the social standing of musical creation.
